concepts.title: Oxidation of Benzene
concepts.conceptBody:
Reactions of Benzene and Its Homologues

(i) Benzene on vigorous oxidation (combustion) gives $\mathbf{C}{{\mathbf{O}}_{\mathbf{2}}}$ and ${{\mathbf{H}}_{\mathbf{2}}}\mathbf{O}$. 

${{\text{C}}_{6}}{{\text{H}}_{6}}(\text{l})+\frac{15}{2}{{\text{O}}_{2}}(~\text{g})\xrightarrow{{}}6\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}(~\text{g})+3{{\text{H}}_{2}}\text{O}(\text{l})$

Benzene on oxidation by air at $723\,\,K\,$ in presence of ${{V}_{2}}{{O}_{5}}$ gives maleic anhydride.

concepts.title: Side Chain Oxidation of Alkyl Benzenes
concepts.conceptBody:
Side chain oxidation of alkyl benzenes 

An alkyl benzenes (irrespective of the chain length) on oxidation by acidified or alkaline$\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}}$(followed by acidification) are degraded to benzoic acid provided they have a benzylic hydrogen.

Even if the side chain contains some functional groups, whether they are electron pushing or electron withdrawing, are also degraded to benzoic acid on oxidation.

are also oxidized by $\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}},{{\text{H}}^{+}}$ or $\text{KMn}{{\text{O}}_{4}},\,\,\,\text{O}{{\text{H}}^{-}}$ to given benzoic acid due to oxidative cleavage of    and  \[-C\equiv C-\]

Compounds that do not contain a benzylic hydrogen will not get oxidised to benzoic acid.

Compounds which contain, two alkyl carbons bearing benzylic hydrogen are oxidised to give two $-\text{C}{{\text{O}}_{2}}\text{H}$ groups on the benzene ring.

concepts.title: Addition Reaction of Benzene
concepts.conceptBody:
Addition reactions: 

Benzene is so stable that it gives addition reactions much less readily. It can be reduced by catalytic hydrogenation, when three molecules of hydrogen add to it in presence of Pt as a catalyst.

Addition of 3 molecules of chlorine also takes place to one molecule of benzene in presence of sunlight. The product formed is 1,2,3,4,5,6-hexachlorocyclohexane (also called as benzene hexachloride). This compound is used as an important insecticide and is sold commercially in the name of$\text{BHC}$ or gammaxene.
